evidence: The paper explicitly uses permutation importance and tree-structure-based approaches, NOT impurity/Gini/MDI importance. Methods section states: "Most of them use the permutation importance that is calculated as the difference of the prediction performance of a variable before and after the permutation of the variable's values." The evaluated methods are Vita (permutation-based with Janitza p-values), Boruta (permutation with shadow variables), and MD/SMD (tree-structure based on minimal depth). No mention of Gini, impurity, or MDI importance appears in the paper. The impurity-importance bias being screened is therefore not applicable.
